group: Already in the product (needs significant refinement)

items.detail: Better visibility into available, active, inherited, and generated context throughout the workflow.
ideas: Wizard allowing superadmin or user to select work type: AI training, catalog, model, attributes, interface, rules, results, BOM, pricing, routing, images
Non-scalar qualitative sufficient context indicator — instead of a percentage or progress bar, show whether context is sufficient for the selected work type
items.title: Context Management

items.detail: Shift emphasis from chat toward durable AI-generated artifacts and their lifecycle.
items.title: Artifact-Centric Workspace

items.detail: Start each work session by defining the goal, available inputs, and recommended workflow.
ideas: Wizard allowing superadmin or user to select work type: AI training, catalog, model, attributes, interface, rules, results, BOM, pricing, routing, images
questions: What are we trying to accomplish today?
What type of work is this?
What can the system help with next?
items.title: Session Planning

items.detail: Automatically classify files, infer scope, identify gaps, and recommend how each should be used.
ideas: From the selected work type, identify what work can be done: AI training, catalog, model, attributes, interface, rules, results, BOM, pricing, routing, images
items.title: Intelligent File Analysis

items.detail: Treat images, drawings, diagrams, and PDFs as first-class inputs alongside text.
questions: What visual information are we ignoring?
How should images, drawings, diagrams, and PDFs contribute to understanding?
items.title: Visual Intelligence

items.detail: Preserve revisions, approvals, and rollback without exposing developer-style version control.
questions: Can I safely iterate without losing previous work?
items.title: Lightweight Versioning

items.detail: Support one-time workspace preparation, context curation, and project setup before implementation begins.
questions: What should be prepared before implementation begins?
What context, assumptions, and project structure should exist before inviting implementation specialists into the workspace?
items.title: Implementation Workspace
